LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT PETSMART, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF PLAINTIFFS FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT REGARDING PRAYER FOR DAMAGES IS OFF CALENDAR. PLAINTIFF ELIZABETH KARNAZES' APPEAL OF THE ORDER VACATING THE DEFAULT JUDGMENT DEPRIVES THIS COURT OF JURISDICTION TO RULE ON THIS MOTION UNTIL THAT APPEAL IS RESOLVED. AN ORDER VACATING A DEFAULT JUDGMENT IS APPEALABLE. (ELSEA V. SABERI (1992) 4 CAL. APP. 4TH 625, 628). SINCE THE COURT OF APPEAL'S DETERMINATION OF THAT APPEAL COULD MOOT THE NEED TO RESOLVE THIS MOTION, THIS MOTION IS EMBRACED IN AND AFFECTED BY THE APPEAL AND THUS THIS COURT LACKS JURISDICTION TO DECIDE THIS MOTION. (VARIAN MEDICAL SYSTEMS, INC.V. DELFINO (2005) 35 CAL. 4TH 180, 189-91). WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: JOSE RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: REYES HUNTER, CSR #6576, BUS. TEL: 530-339-5023. (D302)

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss For Failure To Bring To Trial Within Five Years; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Michael L. Amaro
Off Calendar Apr-27-2020 Off Calendar

Motion To Tax Costs Andor To Strike Petsmart, Inc.S Memorandum Of Costs In Its Entirety, Memorandum Of Points And Authorities, And Declaration Of Elizabeth Karnazes In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Feb-25-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances.

RULING: There being no appearance or objection, the tentative ruling on PLAINTIFF ELIZABETH KARNAZES' Motion To Tax Costs And/Or To Strike Petsmart, Inc.'s Memorandum Of Costs In Its Entirety is adopted as follows:

Plaintiff Elizabeth Karnazes' motion to strike Defendant's costs is granted. The $405.15 claimed by Defendant PetSmart is not adequately supported after a timely challenge by Ms. Karnazes. PetSmart claims $405.15 in costs for "transmitting, filing, and serving of record, briefs, and other papers." "[I]f the items [in a costs memorandum] are properly objected to, they are put in issue and the burden of proof is on the party claiming them as costs." (Sanford v. Rasnick (2016) 246 Cal.App.4th 1121, 1128.) The only costs recoverable on appeal are those specified in Cal. R. Ct. 8.278(d). PetSmart does not provide the court with sufficient documentation to support its claim that it incurred any such recoverable costs. The appeal was dismissed before Ms. Karnazes filed an opening brief, and the Court of Appeal's docket does not reflect that PetSmart filed any records with that court other than sending two letters regarding Plaintiff's notices of unavailability and her requests for extensions of time.

Plaintiff is to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the Court's ruling and submit it in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint Regarding Prayer For Damages
Off Calendar Jul-29-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT PETSMART, INC.'s Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint Regarding Prayer For Damages is OFF CALENDAR. Plaintiff Elizabeth Karnazes' appeal of the order vacating the default judgment deprives this court of jurisdiction to rule on this motion until that appeal is resolved. An order vacating a default judgment is appealable. (Elsea v. Saberi (1992) 4 Cal. App. 4th 625, 628). Since the court of appeal's determination of that appeal could moot the need to resolve this motion, this motion is embraced in and affected by the appeal and thus this court lacks jurisdiction to decide this motion. (Varian Medical Systems, Inc.v. Delfino (2005) 35 Cal. 4th 180, 189-91). Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Reyes Hunter, CSR #6576, Bus. Tel: 530-339-5023. (D302)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint Regarding Prayer For Damages
Off Calendar Jun-22-2016 Continued To Jul-29-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

Motion To Set Aside Default Judgment
Off Calendar Jun-06-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Plaintiff Elizabeth Karnazes telephone number 650-345-9200 appeared by telephone in Propria Persona.
Katherine Agbayani of Amaro Baldwin LLP telephone number 562-912-4157 appeared by telephone for Defendant PetSmart, Inc.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court confirmed its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT PETSMART, INC.'S Motion To Set Aside Default Judgment is granted. While the general rule is that an appeal of a judgment deprives the trial court of jurisdiction to vacate the judgment, all of the case law applying or discussing that general rule is in the context of an appeal filed by the party against whom the judgment was entered seeking to have the judgment vacated or reversed and a concurrent trial court motion to vacate the judgment by the same party or another party against whom the judgment was entered. That is not the context of this case. The appeal was filed by Ms. Karnazes, the party for whom the judgment was entered in her favor and Ms. Karnazes's appeal does not seek to have the default (the predicate to the default judgment) vacated or reversed, but rather she is only appealing the amount of damages awarded to her and she seeks to augment the judgment. In these circumstances, the general rule that an appeal of a judgment deprives the trial court of jurisdiction to vacate the judgment is inapplicable. Instead, the applicable rule is that a trial court motion that is collateral to and does not seek relief that is embraced in or would be affected by the relief that could be granted on the appeal is permissible and within the jurisdiction of the trial court to grant notwithstanding the pendency of the appeal. (CCP 916(a); Varian Medical Systems, Inc. v. Delfino (2005) 35 Cal. 4th 180). This is true even if granting the trial court motion would, as granting Petsmart's motion does here, moot the appeal. (Varian, 35 Cal. 4th at 189). Because there is no possible outcome of the appeal that would affect the granting or denying of Petsmart's motion and that motion will still need to be decided after the appeal is resolved regardless of how it is resolved, this court has jurisdiction to decide Petsmart's motion to vacate the default and default judgment during the pendency of Ms. Karnazes' appeal of the amount of the default judgment. Since Petsmart has shown that it is entitled to have the default and default judgment set aside under CCP 473(b) and 473.5, its motion is granted and the court of appeal should be so advised. Ms. Karnazes' arguments that she served Petsmart, a business organization of unknown form, rather than moving party Petsmart, Inc. does not preclude the granting of this motion. Ms. Karnazes has not shown that there is a Petsmart entity distinct from Petsmart, Inc. that is or could be liable for the damages she seeks nor has she disclaimed an intent to enforce the default judgment against Petsmart, Inc. This ruling makes it unnecessary to decide whether this motion should be granted on the grounds that default judgment is void per CCP 473(d). Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)
